summary: 2026-06-08 18:25:18 PDT â€” Reviewed `git diff main...HEAD`, `git log --oneline main..HEAD`, and `reviews/overlay-teardown-diagnostics.md`. The main offâ†’on recovery path is implemented, but I found one gating edge case and one spec-required test gap. I attempted `npm test`, but this read-only sandbox blocked Vitest from writing its temporary Vite config file (`EPERM`), so the gate was not verifiable here.

findings.title: Stale rearm flag can survive while overlay is hidden
findings.claim: When `needsRearm` is already true and a `video.state === true` event arrives while `overlayOn` is false, the reducer preserves `needsRearm`. Since `App.stopOverlay()` only sets `overlayOn` false and does not clear `needsRearmRef`, a user can hide the overlay after camera-off, then later show it again with a stale pending rearm. A later stray `state:true` event would re-run `startCameraOverlay()` without a fresh offâ†’on transition while the overlay was on, violating AC6's gating requirement.
findings.suggestion: Clear the pending rearm whenever the overlay is off, at least on camera-on events with `overlayOn === false`; also clear `needsRearmRef.current` in `stopOverlay()`. Add a regression test for off while overlay on â†’ hide overlay â†’ camera on â†’ show overlay â†’ stray camera-on does not rearm.

findings.title: AC5 panel recovery behavior is not unit-tested
findings.claim: The branch adds reducer and adapter fan-out tests, but no test exercises the panel effect that actually subscribes to `adapter.onMediaChange`, calls `startCameraOverlay()`, and posts the fresh snapshot. The spec's AC5 test note explicitly requires a unit test that an offâ†’on sequence while `overlayOn` reinvokes `startCameraOverlay()` once and posts a fresh snapshot, so this required behavior can regress while the current tests still pass.
findings.suggestion: Add an App-level test with a fake adapter or `MockZoom`: click/start the overlay, simulate camera off then on, assert one additional `startCameraOverlay()` call, assert a new `postMessage` snapshot, and assert no duplicate rearm without another camera-off.
